title: malaxis verticillata var. pubescens
intro: Malaxis verticillata var. pubescens, also known as the hairy whisker orchid, is a rare and endemic orchid species found in the tropical forests of the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The hairy whisker orchid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Malaxis verticillata var. pubescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its roots and leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
